scores.correctness_notes: Partially correct — identifies certificate_available event and general scenarios for requesting certificates, but misses key gold standard details: revocation handling, request_errors with specific error codes, CSR-based interface contract, and that the library is charmlibs-interfaces-tls-certificates v1
scores.hallucination_notes: TLSCertificatesRequiresV4 and get_assigned_certificate() are not mentioned in the gold standard and may be from an older/different version of the library. The gold standard specifies charmlibs.interfaces.tls_certificates (v1.0+) with a different API model. certificate_available event name is plausible but not confirmed in gold standard which notes event names are part of Python API not the interface spec.
